description: Hierarchical Bayes choice simulator. Runs market simulations from an Excel sim_db â€” share, volume, revenue, sensitivity, and exponent-search analyses.
release_notes:
Initial release of the Choice Simulator plugin for Open Choice.

## Commands

- **sim_run** â€” Standard market simulation. Returns shares, revenue, volume, cost, profit, and standard error per scenario Ã— subgroup Ã— product.
- **sim_sensitivities** â€” Attribute sensitivity sweep. Runs one simulation per (product, attribute, value) triple, stacking all results for chart-ready sensitivity analysis.
- **sim_combinations** â€” Multi-product attribute grid. Cartesian product (expand_grid = true) or zip (expand_grid = false) across value lists for two or more products.
- **sim_take_rate** â€” Take-rate simulation. Identical engine to sim_run; intended for population-projected volume and revenue analyses.
- **sim_exponent_search** â€” IIA exponent calibration. Bisection search over the beta scale factor to match stated market-share proportions.
- **sim_lock_step** â€” Lock-step multi-product sweep. Moves all participating products through parallel value lists simultaneously, one position per simulation run.

## Features

- Reads HB utility draws directly from an Excel sim_db workbook (or pre-built JSON + binary snapshot).
- Share of preference (SoP) and first-choice (FC) probability methods, with blending support.
- Monte Carlo multi-trip simulation with seeded RNG for reproducibility.
- Per-respondent subgroup cuts and multiple weight columns.
- Throttle and consideration set support.
- Eval-lang price formula expressions for derived pricing rules.
- Product net (brand roll-up) rows via include_nets.
- Monotonicity constraints on HB beta draws.
- ocp-json/1 protocol: structured NDJSON event stream with run.started, artifact.created, and run.finished events.
